description: Baseline performance requirements ensuring responsive user experience. Thresholds based on Core Web Vitals and industry standards.

requirements.text: Initial page load (Largest Contentful Paint) completes within 2.5 seconds on a 4G connection, with meaningful content visible within 1 second (First Contentful Paint)
requirements.intent: Users must not wait for the interface to become usable
requirements.verifiable_by: Run Lighthouse or WebPageTest on key pages; confirm LCP ≤2.5s and FCP ≤1s on simulated 4G

requirements.text: UI interactions (clicks, typing, toggling) respond within 100ms — no visible input lag or jank (Interaction to Next Paint ≤200ms)
requirements.intent: The interface must feel instant to the user
requirements.verifiable_by: Run Lighthouse INP audit; confirm INP ≤200ms on key interaction paths

requirements.text: Layout does not shift unexpectedly after initial render (Cumulative Layout Shift ≤0.1)
requirements.intent: Users must not misclick because content moved under their cursor
requirements.verifiable_by: Run Lighthouse CLS audit; confirm CLS ≤0.1 on all pages

requirements.text: API endpoints respond within 200ms at p95 under normal load, and within 1 second at p99
requirements.intent: Backend latency must not become the UX bottleneck
requirements.verifiable_by: Load test API endpoints; confirm p95 ≤200ms and p99 ≤1s under expected traffic

requirements.text: JavaScript bundle size for initial load is under 200KB gzipped, with code splitting for non-critical paths
requirements.intent: Minimize download time and parsing cost on slow connections
requirements.verifiable_by: Check bundle analyzer output; confirm initial bundle ≤200KB gzipped with lazy-loaded routes
